evidence: The problem asks whether A+B 'has positive upper and lower density that are equal', i.e. its natural density; the statement is (A + B).HasPosDensity.
The problem concerns the density of the sumset A+B of the base-3 and base-4 digit-restricted sets.
f(n) counts the distinct prime factors of the product of pairwise sums over an n-element set (docstring: 'at least f(n) distinct prime factors').
The problem asks whether every subgraph density condition forces a triangle, i.e. whether G can be triangle-free (formalized as not G.CliqueFree 3).
Docstring: 'Let G be a triangle-free graph on n vertices ... made into a triangle-free graph with diameter 2 by adding edges'.
The problem asks whether G can be made into a triangle-free graph 'with diameter 2', formalized as every pair of vertices adjacent or joined through a common neighbor.
Docstring: 'Can the product of any k consecutive integers N ever be powerful?', formalized via Nat.Powerful.

The decl states the Erdős–Selfridge theorem [ES75] 'The product of consecutive integers is never a power'; Q18027666 is that number-theory theorem (dewiki 'Satz von Erdős-Selfridge' on this diophantine result).
The variant states the product of k consecutive integers cannot be a perfect power (≠ x^l for l ≥ 2).

The decl asserts that for any r colors and length k some N guarantees a monochromatic arithmetic progression — the docstring calls it 'the fundamental existence result that allows for the definition of the van der Waerden numbers', i.e. van der Waerden's theorem.
The problem asks whether W(k)^{1/k} tends to infinity, where W is the van der Waerden number defined in the file.
W(k) is the threshold for r-colorings of {1,...,N} to contain a monochromatic arithmetic progression of length k.
The decl states r_k(N) = o(N) for the largest AP-free subset of {1,...,N}; erdosproblems.com/139 notes this problem is Szemerédi's theorem (status: proved).
r_k(N) is the largest subset of {1,...,N} with no non-trivial k-term arithmetic progression.
The problem counts integers representable in exactly one way as a sum of two elements of A (the defining property of Sidon/B_2 sets); erdosproblems.com tags 14 with 'sidon sets'.
